programme.desc: An idealistic but obsessive scientist builds a living creature from the body parts of corpses, then abandons his creation to its fate. However, the monster survives, and tracks his creator down to make a sinister request - with terrifying repercussions if his demands are not met. Gothic horror, directed by and starring Kenneth Branagh. With Robert De Niro and Helena Bonham Carter(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Autumn Diaries
programme.desc: Paul Martin investigates an invasive aquatic plant that is clogging up waterways and putting wildlife at risk, and Keeley Donovan visits Gloucester's Woodchester Mansion, home to the largest roost of endangered bats in the country(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of a corner plot in Wallasey, Merseyside, a mid-terrace house in Hyde in Manchester and a bungalow in Great Barr, Birmingham(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A serial fraudster with expensive tastes ends up behind bars after getting caught out by insurers. A claim following a dramatic burglary arouses suspicions(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Two men attacking a jewellers with an axe end up looking less like dangerous criminals and more like comedy characters. Police also close in on the culprits in a series of muggings(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and Danny Sebastian help two teams in Brackley, Northamptonshire, and presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out more about German porcelain(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Becky and Daniel struggle to move on, while Valerie and Ayesha expose a fraud from beyond the grave. Al risks the ire of the Brew Master(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to the North Wessex Downs area of southern England to help a couple to buy their very first home in Britain. Traditional character is high on the pair's wish list, and they have a £500,000 budget to work within. Jules also visits a master clock maker where he examines the delicate inner workings of a 300-year-old timepiece(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades sifts through more junk, taking away a collection of carpet off-cuts, a pair of tired chairs and a bicycle wheel, hoping he and two others can inject new life into them(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Paul Laidlaw scour the Lake District, and while Paul is interested by a 1950s toy, Anita is struck by the poignancy of a Victorian walking stick for children(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Honey puts her foot down when Adam tries to back out of the Queen Vic Halloween party, so he tries to win her back round with the promise of a family holiday. Sharon goes head to head with Mel, armed with her new information, Kheerat and Ash have a showdown as their secret history comes to light and sparks fly between Leo and Whitney at the end of the night. Tiffany hears Jack plotting against her and Keegan(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets midwife and kidney patient Sascha, who has to undergo intense dialysis sessions for hours on end, five days a week. This places the mother-of-four's body under great stress and the constant threat of blood clots gives her a life expectancy of just a few years. Determined to help, the DIY SOS team, bolstered by the local tradespeople and volunteers of Welwyn Garden City, renovate her home to include a dedicated dialysis room, offering her the chance of many more years with her family(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: In Peckham, the body of a 39-year-old man is found slumped in a passageway with a single stab wound to the heart. The documentary follows Met officers as evidence leads them up to a flat where they find six more men, including one lying injured in bed. But is any of them the culprit? Meanwhile, the Community Safety Unit investigate a woman's allegations that she has been emotionally, physically and financially abused by her friend's father, and in Ealing, officers Naomi and Mahad deal with a frequent caller who has called seven times over the past week to complain about her husband(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Birmingham, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason take a look at how things have gone on the day that the October 31st Brexit deadline has been reached(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Autumn Diaries
programme.desc: John Craven explores the stunning countryside around the historic city of Oxford. Paul Martin learns how Queen guitarist Brian May is playing his part in restoring ancient woodland in Dorset, while Margherita Taylor discovers how one of the UK's most scenic autumnal landscapes could be at risk(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts examines a lower ground flat in Pimlico, central London. Martel Maxwell is in Ipswich, Suffolk, and Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, visiting properties bought at auction and both in need of renovation(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: An instance when insurers found themselves dealing with phantom passengers in a two-car shunt. It transpired that opportunists were attempting to make personal injury claims for people that weren't even in the car. Presented by Naga Munchetty(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A couple restoring a house, who had earlier disagreed about installing CCTV, but later found the cameras proved their worth after an intruder invaded their dream home. Plus, the man robbing a pizza takeaway who got a taste of his own medicine when another thief took his bike. Presented by Dom Littlewood(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help two teams competing in Builth Wells, Powys. Presenter Charlie Ross monitors proceedings and also learns about Wales' washing machine industry(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Becky and Daniel's relationship hits a rocky patch, while Karen shares her sadness over Immie, and Jimmi confides in Emma about Daniel. The troops rally to help Jimmi and Al get the Icon shipshape(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Operations manager Kate faces her first challenge, taking on money blogger Jordon on his fourth appearance. She's also up against veteran Andrew, a retired police officer marking his seventh time in the Head Hunter position. As usual, contestants choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves. Quiz show, hosted by Rob Beckett(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to the East Midlands to help a couple find a comfortable country home that brings them closer to family for £450,000. While in the region, Jonnie visits the Leicester Line of the Grand Union canal, a stretch where narrow boats have to navigate a total of 59 locks. He tries his hand at lock keeper's duties, including operating the floodgates(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey for unloved items she can transform. She then turns to upholsterer extraordinaire Sharon O'Connor to make-over a tired armchair, while clockmaker Paul Parry gets creative with a collection of circuit boards. For her own project, Sarah attempts to transform a rusty metal table frame. Narrated by Arthur Smith(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Paul Laidlaw point the classic car to an auction in Newcastle with only a few pounds separating them. A flurry of big bids makes for an exciting last auction for the pair(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic boxing bronze medallist Anthony Ogogo, five-time Paralympic champion Hannah Cockroft, former England rugby union player Ugo Monye and Formula One driver Jolyon Palmer join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, decides the time has come to learn to drive - if only she can find an instructor prepared to give her more than one lesson. Kath organises a bring and buy sale for charity, but Eric is less than happy when he discovers she is planning to put his favourite clothes on her stall. Meanwhile, romance seems to be blossoming for Madge and Gastric, and Willis finally gets away from his mother to go on a date with Ruby. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy and Johnny Vegas star(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Keanu becomes increasingly frustrated with Mel's involvement and admits his concerns to Lisa. Mel's plan begins to unravel and she goes to desperate lengths to ensure she still gets what she wants. Ben lets slip to Karen that Louise and Keanu are moving to Portugal, leaving her devastated. Adam shows his true colours, unaware Mariam is listening to every word he says, leading Habiba and Iqra to hatch a plan to cheer their grandmother up. A furious Tiffany confronts Dotty over her actions(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Jo Brand assumes the role of host as panellists poke fun at the week's events. Comedian Zoe Lyons and columnist, literary editor and drama critic Fintan O'Toole bolster the efforts of regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Actors and comedians Liz Carr and Miles Jupp, actor and writer Samson Kayo and broadcaster Anneka Rice.join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: She's delighted family audiences as Mary Poppins and The Sound of Music's Maria, yet retained the power to shock by appearing in the likes of 10 and A Touch of Adultery. Now singer and actress Julie Andrews is taking stock of her life in Home Work: A Memoir of My Hollywood Years, a follow-up to her critically acclaimed memoir, Home. She appears on Graham's sofa to discuss her life and times. Also appearing are Hollywood stars Reese Witherspoon and Jennifer Aniston, both starring in comedy drama series The Morning Show, and Ian McKellen, who is playing a conman in drama thriller The Good Liar(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Claire and Fleabag return to their father's home for an annual family gathering to mark the anniversary of their mother's death - but this time there is an unwanted guest. Fleabag gets into a confrontation with her godmother, who reveals her plans for a sexually charged art exhibition. Comedy, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: For this week's task, the candidates head to the historic university cities of Oxford and Cambridge to procure nine items that are associated with both places. The added difficulty for the candidates is that they are given no clues as to which of the cities the various items are located in. In Oxford, timewasting leads to a baffling back-and-forth for one team, while in Cambridge, the other team gamble with their negotiations. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Autumn Diaries
programme.desc: Keeley Donovan goes on night patrol with police who are trying to catch criminals stealing contaminated meat, and Margherita Taylor encounters the robot farmers of the future(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern are in Milton Keynes to meet the Howarth family - one of the biggest spending families to ever feature on the programme - to help them change their shopping habits in an attempt to save money. A lack of time management means the family has slid into some bad shopping habits, often buying two or three of the same household items, and having recently renovated their home, the family find themselves with credit card debt. Can Alex and Steph get this family to rein in their spending and get on top of their debt?(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Maureen Gallagher's case reveals the hardships of Germans married to Brits during the wars. An heir to Alan Hughes' estate receives a surprise knock at the door(n)

programme.title: Tom's Midnight Garden (2000)
programme.desc: A boy is sent to live with his elderly aunt and uncle, and discovers a magical hiding place, which appears to him only when the clock strikes 13. He finds the people there cannot see him - except for one girl, with whom he forms a close friendship. Children's drama, based on Philippa Pearce's novel, starring Anthony Way, Florence Hoath, Nigel Le Vaillant, Greta Scacchi, Liz Smith and Joan Plowright(n)

programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts a special celebrity edition of the show as sports commentators Iwan Thomas, Martine Wright, Tania Nadarajah, Sean Rose and Steve Brown compete(n)

programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Nottingham as Carol Gardner and Jane Anderson use their own cash to buy and sell antiques to try to make the most profit(n)

programme.desc: Dev Griffin, Alex Horne, Debbie McGee and June Sarpong test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week(n)

programme.desc: Zoe Ball presents more backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing. Plus, Ian Waite casts his eye over the couples' latest training footage(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests with interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest news on Brexit(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton travels into the secret heart of the Rough Bounds of Knoydart, by way of Loch nan Umbh, Loch Morar, Loch Nevis and Loch Arkaig. He lifts the lid on the area's secret connection with the celebrated SOE (Special Operations Executive), which Churchill hoped would 'set Europe ablaze'(n)

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams present live from the Cairngorms National Park, where overnight events have already created dramas. The team look at how this year's seasonal changes have shaped Autumn 2019. Meanwhile, Michaela embarks on a wild goose chase, and the camera team are on a mission to find a deer rut deep in the Abernethy forest(n)

programme.title: Guilt
programme.desc: In a residential Edinburgh street one night, two brothers run into and kill an old man. Uninsured and under the influence, record shop owner Jake persuades high-flying lawyer Max to cover up the act. But their actions soon come back to haunt them. Thriller, starring Mark Bonnar and Jamie Sives, with Emun Elliott and Sian Brooke(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice: You're Fired
programme.desc: The companion programme to The Apprentice, with Tom Allen talking to the fifth candidate to be ejected from the competition. The host is also joined by a panel of experts and fans, who offer an entertaining analysis of this week's task(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: The Fall
programme.desc: A mob's punishment of a lone man proves cruel and unusual in this nightmarish short film, written and directed by Jonathan Glazer(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass investigate a gang-related murder on Clapham Common in 1953 that left 17-year-old John Beckley dead. Several young men were initially arrested and charged, only one - Michael Davies - was found guilty of murder and sentenced to death. Michael's punishment was reduced to life imprisonment and now, more than six decades later, his niece, Sharon is looking for closure. Michael protested his innocence on his release from prison, but he was never exonerated - can his relative finally clear his name?(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon reaches the United States, travelling down the Rocky Mountains from Montana to Colorado. In the wide-open prairies of Montana, the cowboy way of life is under threat from a long-term decline in the rural economy, but Simon meets a former Silicon Valley executive who has envisaged a new future for the land. In a country with the highest incarceration rate in the world, Simon's final destination on this leg is a Colorado town that depends almost entirely on 11 prisons. Simon witnesses how inmates are put to work for the state, and how virtual reality is being used to prepare prisoners for life on the outside(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Former Dragon Theo Paphitis returns to the panel to stand in for Touker Suleyman, joining Sara Davies, Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden and Tej Lalvani in assessing more business ideas. This week's entrepreneurs pitch their ideas for a diet app, a clothes-mending workshop, a gadget for repairing bicycle chains, and a range of dog treats(n)

programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey for unloved items she can transform, including an old oak table and Victorian sofa(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Autumn Diaries
programme.desc: Keeley Donovan returns to the site of the devastating fires on Marsden moors, while Steve Brown digs into the history of the autumnal fruit, the wild blackberry(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Morden Hall Park in south London, where the experts asses items including a Faberge vanity case, a ring by one of the most innovative goldsmiths of the 20th century and a magnifying glass linked to the Pablo Picasso exhibition in New York in 1939. Lennox Cato faces a puzzle when he appraises a pair of antique doors belonging to the local mosque and jewellery specialist Jon Benjamin gets his hands on the largest aquamarine he has ever seen(n)

programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Theo Randall puts three amateur cooks through a series of culinary challenges, including how to make linguine asparagus carbonara, choosing one of them to join him in the final(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers are on the rock in Ireland, searching for the beneficiaries to the estate of a Limerick-born man. Another case reveals the compulsive hoarding habits of a car enthusiast, and uncovers a potentially highly valuable collection of Dinky toys. Meanwhile, the work of the team brings about an emotional family reunion(n)

programme.title: King Kong (1933)
programme.desc: A film producer and his crew visit a remote island where they encounter a 50ft gorilla which takes a shine to their leading lady. When they decide to take it back to America, they fail to foresee the inevitable havoc as the gigantic ape runs amok in New York. Fantasy, starring Fay Wray, Robert Armstrong and Bruce Cabot(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Consumer journalists Jasmine Birtles, Chris Hollins, Simon Calder, Adam Pearson and Rebecca Wilcox compete in a charity edition hosted by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Nottingham, where vintage fan Melissa Mason and jewellery designer Paolo Moscardini learn the tricks of the buying trade to see who can make the most profit(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Dev Griffin, Alex Horne, Debbie McGee and June Sarpong test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball has more backstage gossip, gets a sneak peek at some of Saturday's costumes with head designer Vicky Gill and Ian Waite analyses the celebrities' training clips(n)

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas is joined by Craig Mitch, Chelcee Grimes and Reece Parkinson to take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: More than a billion years ago, the far north of Sutherland was struck by a meteorite and though the wreckage of this cataclysmic event has been almost completely worn away by time, the rocks in the landscape still bear some traces. Paul Murton examines these as he embarks on a journey from Lairg on Loch Shin to Lochinver and finally to the summit of Suilven - one of the most distinctive mountains in Scotland(n)

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams return with more wildlife reports live from the Cairngorms National Park. For Halloween, they reveal the secrets of Troll Mountain on the Island of Rum, Chris reveals the surprising science inside an antler and there is a search for Britain's biggest spider(n)

programme.title: Giri/Haji
programme.desc: Kenzo is finally reunited with Yuto, and while he gets some answers, any hopes of a reconciliation are dashed when he finds himself back at square one, still chasing after his brother and mystified by his anger. A surprise visitor from Tokyo lifts the detective's spirits, although he still has the threat of crime boss Abbot's threat hanging over his head. However, during an evening of confessions with Sarah, both question whether Yuto is the only reason he has not returned home yet. Crime thriller, starring Takehiro Hira, Yuko Nakamora and Kelly Macdonald(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools presents another selection of music, introducing Manchester's finest Elbow with a track from their eighth album Giants of All Sizes, Kasabian's Serge Pizzorno with his solo project The SLP, which combines rock guitars and dance music, and folk-rock singer Michael Kiwanuka, with music from his self-titled third album. Completing the line-up are two acts shortlisted for the 2019 Mercury Prize - north London rapper Little Simz and rapper Slowthai, making his debut on the show. The presenter also chats to Birmingham MC Lady Leshurr, who co-hosts tomorrow's show, and comedian James Acaster(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis and Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: A Dangerous Dynasty: House of Assad
programme.desc: Documentary about the al-Assad family, which has ruled Syria since 1971 and established an authoritarian government in the country under the control of the Ba'ath Party. Current Syrian president Bashar al-Assad trained to be an eye doctor at St Mary's Hospital in Paddington, London, and his wife Asma was born in Britain to a Syrian family. Told through rarely-seen footage and testimony of those who knew and worked with them, the series explores how the couple, who at first were seen as a modernising force, ended up running a regime accused of war crimes(n)

programme.title: Anna: The Woman Who Went to Fight ISIS
programme.desc: In 2017, 25-year-old Anna Campbell from Lewes in East Sussex travelled in secret to Syria. She was heading for Rojava, the Kurdish territory in the north of the country. In the midst of the civil war in Syria, a fledgling feminist democracy had been established, but almost immediately came under threat from the so-called Islamic State. Just eight months after arriving and with no military background, Anna went to the front line to fight with Kurdish Women's Protection Units (YPJ). A month later she was killed by a Turkish air strike. With access to her diary and videos filmed while she was there, this documentary explores what motivated Anna to leave and how her family are making sense of the tragic consequences(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Drug dealers collect billions of pounds in the UK every year, but how do they get their dirty money into the financial system? Reporter Andy Verity follows the criminal cash from the streets of London to the gold markets of Dubai, revealing how an international crime gang laundered drug money around the world and how bankers and accountants at two big city firms failed to stop it(n)

programme.title: Million Dollar Wedding Planner
programme.desc: The exclusive world of Lelian Chew, who organises nuptials for the super-rich in the Far East, taking on 10 just ceremonies a year at an average cost of $1million each. Bespoke Cartier jewellery, hundreds of thousands of dollars of flowers and multiple designer wedding dresses are nothing out of the ordinary. But Lelian's job also involves navigating the clash between age-old Chinese marriage customs and the desires of the young, often with outlandish solutions - multiple weddings are becoming the norm for the Chinese super-rich(n)

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three artists to create artwork for mystery buyers who are looking for a joyous, uplifting piece in light of a recent cancer diagnosis(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two

programme.desc: Paul Martin investigates an invasive aquatic plant that is clogging up waterways and putting wildlife at risk, and Keeley Donovan visits Gloucester's Woodchester Mansion, home to the largest roost of endangered bats in the country(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Mary Berry comes to tea with Monty Don and, as well has giving her a tour of Longmeadow, he offers his advice on her new garden. Frances Tophill meets gardeners on an allotment in Birmingham and harvests some unusual vegetables, while Adam Frost and Arit Anderson join forces to transform a front garden in Hampshire into a haven for the owners as well as wildlife. In Nottinghamshire, the programme meets an enthusiast who has amassed a collection of over 100 varieties of rhubarb and in Scotland a herb grower with over 600 different types(n)

programme.title: Yes Chef

And to pile on the pressure, three-star Michelin chef Pierre Koffmann judges their final dishes. Culinary challenge, hosted by Sheree Murphy(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: After the detectives' hard work, a person visits the home of a relative she didn't know existed, and who surprisingly lives just 10 minutes' from her own house. On another case, the hunters experience a strange sense of deja vu when they trace heirs to the estate of a woman they have met before, and their search uncovers the remarkable history of Thames bargemen. Plus, details of more unclaimed estates are divulged(n)

programme.title: Streetdance (2010)
programme.desc: A street dance crew trains for a national competition, only to find themselves forced to share rehearsal rooms with a ballet company. While the two groups take an instant dislike to one another, the conflict fades over time and they make plans to combine their different styles into a new form of modern dance. Drama, with Nichola Burley, Richard Winsor and Charlotte Rampling(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts as children's TV presenters Hayley Hassall, Martin Dougan, Radzi Chinyanganya, Ayshah Tull and Katie Thistleton compete for charity(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: In Exeter, former air traffic controller Paul Johnson takes on behavioural safety consultant Keith Jackson in the antiques challenge. Along the way presenter Paul Martin teaches them the tricks of the trade and the dos and don'ts of how to be an antique dealer(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal present analysis of the pro-celebrity couples' progress ahead of tomorrow night's show. Celebrity super fans discuss all things ballroom, Gethin Jones catches up with the couples backstage at this week's Strictly rehearsals, and there's a live music performance in the studio(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Radio host Dev Griffin, comedian Alex Horne, presenter June Sarpong and the lovely Debbie McGee go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys grills four more contenders on their chosen subjects, which are Greek mythology, Manchester City FC, American novelist Harper Lee and EF Benson's Mapp and Lucia novels. This is followed by a two-minute general knowledge round(n)

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams end their week-long adventure in the Cairngorms, looking at the autumns of the future and discovering encouraging news about Scottish wildcats. Gillian shares her amazing encounter with an 8ft-long blue shark, and there's an update on Cairngorms Connect, Britain's Great Landscape restoration project(n)

programme.title: The Name of the Rose
programme.desc: William and Adso find Berengar dead, and when Severinus discovers William's missing lenses in the late man's habit, William is sure they have the identity of Adso's attacker. The Franciscan and Papal envoys arrive for the conference, but bring news the Pope is sending Bernard Gui, the grand inquisitor, as his envoy. William and Adso continue their search for a secret chamber, the Finis Africae, and find various mechanisms to deter intruders. Adso is distracted by the presence of the strange Occitan girl in the forest, who is also the object of desire of the strange and demented Salvatore(n)

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti work in Ireland's Ashford Castle. Set in 350 acres, it was founded 800 years ago by the Anglo-Norman De Burgo family, and much later became the family home of the Guinness family. With the help of hotel historian, Fintan, Giles discovers that the castle has had a rollercoaster journey through the centuries, sporadically reaching moments of real jeopardy, only to be saved by a turn of events or new owners. Giles and Monica soon learn that the secret of the castle's success is the people who work here and the hotel's strong, long-running community ties(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Eshaan Akbar, Angela Barnes, Ed Gamble, Rhys James and Felicity Ward and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Extended edition of the music programme, featuring Birmingham rapper and freestyle queen Lady Leshurr as co-host. Manchester's finest Elbow perform a track from their eighth album Giants of All Sizes, Kasabian's Serge Pizzorno presents his solo project the SLP, which combines rock guitars and dance music and folk-rock singer Michael Kiwanuka offers music from his self-titled third album. Completing the line-up are two acts shortlisted for the 2019 Mercury Prize - north London rapper Little Simz and rapper Slowthai, making his debut on the show. Jools also finds time to chat to comedian James Acaster(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: A Stitch in Time
programme.desc: Amber Butchart explores the lives of historical figures through the clothes they wore, this week providing a portrait of a working man(n)

programme.title: Six Wives with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: A dramatised account of the key moments in the lives of Henry VIII's wives, combining drama with the presenter's own contemporary historical comment. In this episode, Lucy follows Catherine of Aragon through her emotional and physical struggles to give Henry the heir he so desperately needs to continue his dynasty. When Catherine finally gives birth to a baby girl, Henry's eye wanders to the ladies at court - first Mary Boleyn, and then to her sister, Anne(n)

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Art experts Emma Dabiri and Dr Bendor Grosvenor visit museums as they track down previously unknown paintings by some of the world's greatest artists. In Oxford's Bodleian Library, Dr Bendor Grosvenor finds a finely painted portrait of Merton College fellow George Oakley Aldrich by an unknown artist, which he believes could be a piece of work by Pompeo Batoni(n)

programme.title: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Historian Janina Ramirez and art critic Alastair Sooke embark on cultural city breaks, offering insights into the most famous locations and uncovering hidden gems and untold stories to reveal how religion, revolution and trailblazing individuals can shape the art - and soul - of a metropolis. They begin by examining the art and design of the Dutch capital Amsterdam, along the way discovering how its culture reflects the liberal attitudes, appetite for global trade and love of home comforts that helped shape the character of this city(n)

programme.title: Queen Victoria's Letters: A Monarch Unveiled
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part programme, biographer AN Wilson explores the personal life of Queen Victoria through her journals and letters, examining the monarch's closest relationships in the four decades following Prince Albert's death to chart how her life changed. He looks at her controversial friendship with her servant John Brown and her unconventional behaviour around Indian waiter Abdul Karim, and also reveals how she gained greater freedom in her widowhood. Includes readings by Anna Chancellor(n)

programme.title: Francesco's Venice
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto investigates the truth behind the sinking of Venice and discusses the likely long-term effect of a current exodus of inhabitants leaving to find jobs elsewhere. He explains how 19th-century British artists and writers, including Turner and Dickens, wove a romantic myth around the city, and describes his family's connection to one sexually promiscuous figure(n)

programme.title: The Renaissance Unchained
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak challenges received wisdom about the cultural movement that began in the Late Medieval period. He begins by examining the notion of the Renaissance having fixed origins in Italy, showcasing the ingenuity in both technique and ideas of great artists including Van Eyck, Memling, Van der Weyden, Cranach, Riemenschneider and Durer(n)

programme.title: Six Wives with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: A dramatised account of the key moments in the lives of Henry VIII's wives, combining drama with the presenter's own contemporary historical comment. In this episode, Lucy follows Catherine of Aragon through her emotional and physical struggles to give Henry the heir he so desperately needs to continue his dynasty. When Catherine finally gives birth to a baby girl, Henry's eye wanders to the ladies at court - first Mary Boleyn, and then to her sister, Anne(n)

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Art experts Emma Dabiri and Dr Bendor Grosvenor visit museums as they track down previously unknown paintings by some of the world's greatest artists. In Oxford's Bodleian Library, Dr Bendor Grosvenor finds a finely painted portrait of Merton College fellow George Oakley Aldrich by an unknown artist, which he believes could be a piece of work by Pompeo Batoni(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Mike Read and Andy Crane present the edition first shown on September 8, featuring performances by Marc Almond, Gloria Estefan and Miami Sound Machine, Spagna, Anthrax, Bill Medley, the Proclaimers, Michael Jackson, Yello, Phil Collins and Jane Wiedlin(n)

programme.title: Forces of Nature with Brian Cox
programme.desc: The physicist concludes his journey by examining the effects of light interacting with the Earth, travelling to Iceland to witness a moonbow - the band of colours released when moonbeams are refracted by the spray of a waterfall. He then visits the site of an enormous outpouring of lava to explain why hot things glow red, and reveals how the different colours of light energy from the sun transform the plains of the Serengeti in Tanzania and drive the migration of humpback whales to the Caribbean each year(n)

programme.title: The Secrets of Quantum Physics
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili continues his exploration of quantum physics, asking whether the subject can be used to solve mysteries in biology, looking at how the European robin navigates using the process of quantum entanglement, and how humans use quantum vibrations in their sense of smell. Finally, the presenter looks at the Uncertainty Principle, and surmises that the effects suggested by this science might play a role in evolution(n)

programme.title: The Dead Room
programme.desc: Ghost story written and directed by Mark Gatiss that is set in the studios of a long-running radio horror series. The presenter of the show (Simon Callow) resents the fact that he must adapt to changing times and tastes, but during an eerie night's work, he begins to suspect that dark forces are at work in the studio and his own past may not be as buried as he expected. With Anjli Mohindra, Susan Penhaligon and Joshua Oakes-Rogers(n)

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic dissects his favourite genre, horror, and reveals how film makers exploit peoples' darkest fears. Featuring examples from Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, Jordan Peele's surprise blockbuster Get Out, and the movie which has become Mark Kermode's lifetime's obsession, William Friedkin's The Exorcist(n)

programme.title: Pain, Pus & Poison: The Search for Modern Medicines
programme.desc: Dr Michael Mosley's three-part documentary exploring the history of medicine concludes as he tells the story of how scientists went from finding antidotes to poisons to applying otherwise dangerous toxins as a cure. He reveals why he believes, of all the natural remedies featured in his scientific tour, the tales of treatments derived from deadly substances are perhaps the most extraordinary he encountered(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Mike Read and Andy Crane present the edition first shown on September 8, featuring performances by Marc Almond, Gloria Estefan and Miami Sound Machine, Spagna, Anthrax, Bill Medley, the Proclaimers, Michael Jackson, Yello, Phil Collins and Jane Wiedlin(n)

programme.title: Africa's Great Civilisations
programme.desc: Henry Louis Gates Jr travels the length and breadth of Africa to explore the continent's epic and revelatory history. The first episode examines the origins of human existence and the anthropological and scientific discoveries that point to Africa as the genetic home of all currently living humanity(n)

programme.title: Africa's Great Civilisations
programme.desc: Henry Louis Gates Jr reveals how Christianity and Islam reshaped the landscape and people of Africa between the first and 12th centuries AD and for hundreds of years to come(n)

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: An exploration of Australia's underwater ecosystem, one of the natural wonders of the world and the largest living structure on the planet. In the first of three programmes, marine biologist Monty Halls examines the complex form of the coral reef itself and the wildlife that lives on it, from deadly fish-hunting snails and sharks that can walk on land to fighting corals and parrotfish, which cocoon themselves every night(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Exotic Pop at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of archive performances by international artists who found success in the UK charts, including Greek crooner Demis Roussos, French star Vanessa Paradis, Swedish singer Sylvia and Romanian pan-pipe player Gheorghe Zamfir(n)

programme.title: Get Rich or Try Dying: Music's Mega Legacies
programme.desc: Death has its obvious downside, but in the world of pop, shuffling off the mortal coil can offer a unique business opportunity. Last year, the top five estates of the most cherished but deceased popstars earned $0.5billion. Ana Matronic embarks on a journey into the afterlife of pop, discovering the many ways pop stars earn a living when they're dead. Contributors including agents, publicists, producers and families reveal the dos and don'ts of keeping the dream alive(n)

Archie tries to fix the soapy situation, but soon there are bubbles all over town. Bobby the Vet and Newsreader Simon try to help, but Biggelton is in big bubble trouble. Finally, Hairdresser Hayley realises that Archie can save the day(n)

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Robert hopes to attend the Great Robot Ball, but needs to find someone to take care of Justin and Little Monster. Children's entertainment, featuring singing, dancing and comedy(n)

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Mo needs to go to the shops, but Tee is more interested in building a rocket out of cardboard boxes(n)

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘bubble' and we go on a whirlwind word adventure(n)

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Timmy steals Ruffy's beret, Otus's glove-puppet and Mittens' wagon - but does not know what to do when something of his own goes missing(n)

programme.desc: Bobble builds a hotel for the Scuttlies, but it's too well made! They make Scooper Dooper, who transforms it, and builds a bigger one for the Olobobs to have a holibob too(n)

programme.title: Bing

programme.desc: The gang decides to cover things after seeing a tablecloth. Meanwhile, everyone gets covered in spots back on the boat(n)

programme.desc: For this week's task, the candidates head to the historic university cities of Oxford and Cambridge to procure nine items that are associated with both places. programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A serial fraudster with expensive tastes ends up behind bars after getting caught out by insurers. A claim following a dramatic burglary arouses suspicions(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to the North Wessex Downs area of southern England to help a couple to buy their very first home in Britain. Traditional character is high on the pair's wish list, and they have a £500,000 budget to work within. Jules also visits a master clock maker where he examines the delicate inner workings of a 300-year-old timepiece(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades sifts through more junk, taking away a collection of carpet off-cuts, a pair of tired chairs and a bicycle wheel, hoping he and two others can inject new life into them(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Paul Laidlaw scour the Lake District, and while Paul is interested by a 1950s toy, Anita is struck by the poignancy of a Victorian walking stick for children(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Honey puts her foot down when Adam tries to back out of the Queen Vic Halloween party, so he tries to win her back round with the promise of a family holiday. Sharon goes head to head with Mel, armed with her new information, Kheerat and Ash have a showdown as their secret history comes to light and sparks fly between Leo and Whitney at the end of the night. Tiffany hears Jack plotting against her and Keegan(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets midwife and kidney patient Sascha, who has to undergo intense dialysis sessions for hours on end, five days a week. This places the mother-of-four's body under great stress and the constant threat of blood clots gives her a life expectancy of just a few years. Determined to help, the DIY SOS team, bolstered by the local tradespeople and volunteers of Welwyn Garden City, renovate her home to include a dedicated dialysis room, offering her the chance of many more years with her family(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: In Peckham, the body of a 39-year-old man is found slumped in a passageway with a single stab wound to the heart. The documentary follows Met officers as evidence leads them up to a flat where they find six more men, including one lying injured in bed. But is any of them the culprit? Meanwhile, the Community Safety Unit investigate a woman's allegations that she has been emotionally, physically and financially abused by her friend's father, and in Ealing, officers Naomi and Mahad deal with a frequent caller who has called seven times over the past week to complain about her husband(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Birmingham, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason take a look at how things have gone on the day that the October 31st Brexit deadline has been reached(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Autumn Diaries
programme.desc: John Craven explores the stunning countryside around the historic city of Oxford. Paul Martin learns how Queen guitarist Brian May is playing his part in restoring ancient woodland in Dorset, while Margherita Taylor discovers how one of the UK's most scenic autumnal landscapes could be at risk(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts examines a lower ground flat in Pimlico, central London. Martel Maxwell is in Ipswich, Suffolk, and Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, visiting properties bought at auction and both in need of renovation(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: An instance when insurers found themselves dealing with phantom passengers in a two-car shunt. It transpired that opportunists were attempting to make personal injury claims for people that weren't even in the car. Presented by Naga Munchetty(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A couple restoring a house, who had earlier disagreed about installing CCTV, but later found the cameras proved their worth after an intruder invaded their dream home. Plus, the man robbing a pizza takeaway who got a taste of his own medicine when another thief took his bike. Presented by Dom Littlewood(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help two teams competing in Builth Wells, Powys. Presenter Charlie Ross monitors proceedings and also learns about Wales' washing machine industry(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Becky and Daniel's relationship hits a rocky patch, while Karen shares her sadness over Immie, and Jimmi confides in Emma about Daniel. The troops rally to help Jimmi and Al get the Icon shipshape(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Operations manager Kate faces her first challenge, taking on money blogger Jordon on his fourth appearance. She's also up against veteran Andrew, a retired police officer marking his seventh time in the Head Hunter position. As usual, contestants choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves. Quiz show, hosted by Rob Beckett(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to the East Midlands to help a couple find a comfortable country home that brings them closer to family for £450,000. While in the region, Jonnie visits the Leicester Line of the Grand Union canal, a stretch where narrow boats have to navigate a total of 59 locks. He tries his hand at lock keeper's duties, including operating the floodgates(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey for unloved items she can transform. She then turns to upholsterer extraordinaire Sharon O'Connor to make-over a tired armchair, while clockmaker Paul Parry gets creative with a collection of circuit boards. For her own project, Sarah attempts to transform a rusty metal table frame. Narrated by Arthur Smith(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Paul Laidlaw point the classic car to an auction in Newcastle with only a few pounds separating them. A flurry of big bids makes for an exciting last auction for the pair(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure
programme.desc: The rugby legend and wife Maureen travel along the Monmouthshire and Brecon canal, sampling the breath-taking views of the Brecon Beacons. Determined to show that he has mastered the art of narrow-boating, Gareth takes charge of the Bentley of Barges. As a reward for their endeavours, the pair discover how to make gin with ingredients found in the hedgerow. When the canal passes the Glanusk Estate, Gareth seizes the chance to enjoy a spot of fly fishing. Later, in Brecon, they enjoy the performance of a jazz band - a style of music with which the town is synonymous thanks to its annual festival(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, decides the time has come to learn to drive - if only she can find an instructor prepared to give her more than one lesson. Kath organises a bring and buy sale for charity, but Eric is less than happy when he discovers she is planning to put his favourite clothes on her stall. Meanwhile, romance seems to be blossoming for Madge and Gastric, and Willis finally gets away from his mother to go on a date with Ruby. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy and Johnny Vegas star(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Keanu becomes increasingly frustrated with Mel's involvement and admits his concerns to Lisa. Mel's plan begins to unravel and she goes to desperate lengths to ensure she still gets what she wants. Ben lets slip to Karen that Louise and Keanu are moving to Portugal, leaving her devastated. Adam shows his true colours, unaware Mariam is listening to every word he says, leading Habiba and Iqra to hatch a plan to cheer their grandmother up. A furious Tiffany confronts Dotty over her actions(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Jo Brand assumes the role of host as panellists poke fun at the week's events. Comedian Zoe Lyons and columnist, literary editor and drama critic Fintan O'Toole bolster the efforts of regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Actors and comedians Liz Carr and Miles Jupp, actor and writer Samson Kayo and broadcaster Anneka Rice.join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: She's delighted family audiences as Mary Poppins and The Sound of Music's Maria, yet retained the power to shock by appearing in the likes of 10 and A Touch of Adultery. Now singer and actress Julie Andrews is taking stock of her life in Home Work: A Memoir of My Hollywood Years, a follow-up to her critically acclaimed memoir, Home. She appears on Graham's sofa to discuss her life and times. Also appearing are Hollywood stars Reese Witherspoon and Jennifer Aniston, both starring in comedy drama series The Morning Show, and Ian McKellen, who is playing a conman in drama thriller The Good Liar(n)

programme.title: Tourist Trap
programme.desc: The country is gripped by Banksy fever following the discovery of one of his artworks in Port Talbot, while the staff of Wow Wales! settle into their new premises. Return of the comedy about a tourism agency, starring Sally Phillips(n)

programme.title: Beauty Queen and Single
programme.desc: Miss UK Beauty first runner-up 2015 Alaw is seeking someone special. She's conscious her parents would love her to find a Welsh speaker as that is their family's first language. Standing at just 4ft 11ins, Alaw's got a tall boy on her wish-list. She admits she comes across as confident on the outside but is a bag of nerves on the inside - and that's exactly how she feels as the make-under begins. Like all the beauty queens, Alaw's supported by stylist Nathan who provides the voice of reason and a personal twist on a simple black dress bespoke to each girls(n)

programme.title: The Edit
programme.desc: The best of the week's entertainment news with Amy Irons(n)

Jules also visits a master clock maker where he examines the delicate inner workings of a 300-year-old timepiece(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Honey puts her foot down when Adam tries to back out of the Queen Vic Halloween party, so he tries to win her back round with the promise of a family holiday. Sharon goes head to head with Mel, armed with her new information, Kheerat and Ash have a showdown as their secret history comes to light and sparks fly between Leo and Whitney at the end of the night. Tiffany hears Jack plotting against her and Keegan(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets midwife and kidney patient Sascha, who has to undergo intense dialysis sessions for hours on end, five days a week. This places the mother-of-four's body under great stress and the constant threat of blood clots gives her a life expectancy of just a few years. Determined to help, the DIY SOS team, bolstered by the local tradespeople and volunteers of Welwyn Garden City, renovate her home to include a dedicated dialysis room, offering her the chance of many more years with her family(n)

programme.title: Laid Bare
programme.desc: Airline shift-worker Victoria can make up to four flights a day, as a result of which she eats at irregular hours and suffers from constant fatigue and complains about regular stomach pain. Meanwhile, Johan works as a successful chef, and despite serving up culinary treats for others, his own eating habits are poor and he gets next to no exercise, meaning he is gaining weight. The two of them undergo tests to find out how their health is being affected, before being given an eight-week programme in an attempt to turn their lives around(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Birmingham, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason take a look at how things have gone on the day that the October 31st Brexit deadline has been reached(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Autumn Diaries
programme.desc: John Craven explores the stunning countryside around the historic city of Oxford. Paul Martin learns how Queen guitarist Brian May is playing his part in restoring ancient woodland in Dorset, while Margherita Taylor discovers how one of the UK's most scenic autumnal landscapes could be at risk(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts examines a lower ground flat in Pimlico, central London. Martel Maxwell is in Ipswich, Suffolk, and Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, visiting properties bought at auction and both in need of renovation(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: An instance when insurers found themselves dealing with phantom passengers in a two-car shunt. It transpired that opportunists were attempting to make personal injury claims for people that weren't even in the car. Presented by Naga Munchetty(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A couple restoring a house, who had earlier disagreed about installing CCTV, but later found the cameras proved their worth after an intruder invaded their dream home. Plus, the man robbing a pizza takeaway who got a taste of his own medicine when another thief took his bike. Presented by Dom Littlewood(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help two teams competing in Builth Wells, Powys. Presenter Charlie Ross monitors proceedings and also learns about Wales' washing machine industry(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Becky and Daniel's relationship hits a rocky patch, while Karen shares her sadness over Immie, and Jimmi confides in Emma about Daniel. The troops rally to help Jimmi and Al get the Icon shipshape(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Operations manager Kate faces her first challenge, taking on money blogger Jordon on his fourth appearance. She's also up against veteran Andrew, a retired police officer marking his seventh time in the Head Hunter position. As usual, contestants choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves. Quiz show, hosted by Rob Beckett(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to the East Midlands to help a couple find a comfortable country home that brings them closer to family for £450,000. While in the region, Jonnie visits the Leicester Line of the Grand Union canal, a stretch where narrow boats have to navigate a total of 59 locks. He tries his hand at lock keeper's duties, including operating the floodgates(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey for unloved items she can transform. She then turns to upholsterer extraordinaire Sharon O'Connor to make-over a tired armchair, while clockmaker Paul Parry gets creative with a collection of circuit boards. For her own project, Sarah attempts to transform a rusty metal table frame. Narrated by Arthur Smith(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Paul Laidlaw point the classic car to an auction in Newcastle with only a few pounds separating them. A flurry of big bids makes for an exciting last auction for the pair(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Test Drive
programme.desc: Grado hosts the car-based game show in which three teams answer questions via a specially-adapted satnav that will determine their route to a well-known tourist attraction, this time Linlithgow. Correct responses mean they will follow the shortest route, but if they are wrong there will be diversions, wrong turns and miles added to their trip(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, decides the time has come to learn to drive - if only she can find an instructor prepared to give her more than one lesson. Kath organises a bring and buy sale for charity, but Eric is less than happy when he discovers she is planning to put his favourite clothes on her stall. Meanwhile, romance seems to be blossoming for Madge and Gastric, and Willis finally gets away from his mother to go on a date with Ruby. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy and Johnny Vegas star(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Keanu becomes increasingly frustrated with Mel's involvement and admits his concerns to Lisa. Mel's plan begins to unravel and she goes to desperate lengths to ensure she still gets what she wants. Ben lets slip to Karen that Louise and Keanu are moving to Portugal, leaving her devastated. Adam shows his true colours, unaware Mariam is listening to every word he says, leading Habiba and Iqra to hatch a plan to cheer their grandmother up. A furious Tiffany confronts Dotty over her actions(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Jo Brand assumes the role of host as panellists poke fun at the week's events. Comedian Zoe Lyons and columnist, literary editor and drama critic Fintan O'Toole bolster the efforts of regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Actors and comedians Liz Carr and Miles Jupp, actor and writer Samson Kayo and broadcaster Anneka Rice.join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: She's delighted family audiences as Mary Poppins and The Sound of Music's Maria, yet retained the power to shock by appearing in the likes of 10 and A Touch of Adultery. Now singer and actress Julie Andrews is taking stock of her life in Home Work: A Memoir of My Hollywood Years, a follow-up to her critically acclaimed memoir, Home. She appears on Graham's sofa to discuss her life and times. Also appearing are Hollywood stars Reese Witherspoon and Jennifer Aniston, both starring in comedy drama series The Morning Show, and Ian McKellen, who is playing a conman in drama thriller The Good Liar(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic boxing bronze medallist Anthony Ogogo, five-time Paralympic champion Hannah Cockroft, former England rugby union player Ugo Monye and Formula One driver Jolyon Palmer join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: A View from the Terrace
programme.desc: Craig G Telfer, Craig Fowler, Shaughan McGuigan and Joel Sked present a sideways look at Scottish football, dissecting of ins and outs of the week's action, with a variety of features covering the full spectrum from the giants to the minnows. Plus, Robert Borthwick presents to the panel his favourite clips, comments, GIFS and antics from the world of social media, whether that's from fans, players, teams or managers(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Autumn Diaries
programme.desc: Keeley Donovan returns to the site of the devastating fires on Marsden moors, while Steve Brown digs into the history of the autumnal fruit, the wild blackberry(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell looks round a three-bedroom house in Worcester and Dion Dublin sees a tiny basement flat in Liverpool. Plus, a revisit to a London property first featured in 2016(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: An audacious claim for compensation ends up costing one man a lot more than he bargained for when CCTV shows what really happened(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A shop owner risks her life to save a colleague from an armed robber wielding an axe. Elsewhere, two teenagers steal a life size ornamental pig(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents from Leominster in Herefordshire, where experts Caroline Hawley and Thomas Plant advise two teams competing to win the greater profit at auction(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara uncovers a twisted secret when she treats two pregnant friends. Elsewhere, Daniel and Becky's joy turns to disappointment(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants Yasmin, Michael and Dubem must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple with a £600,000 budget search for a country home in Dorset with the potential to generate income. Away from the hunt, the presenter discovers how to make a traditional shepherd's hut(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey for unloved items she can transform, including an old oak table and Victorian sofa(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Paul Laidlaw search for antiques to sell at auction in Cumbria. Anita comes across a Victorian mechanical pencil, and Paul finds a watch from the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio(n)

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Ten well-known actors come together to record an album for Children in Need. Cameras follow Helena Bonham Carter, Jim Broadbent, Olivia Colman, Shaun Dooley, Luke Evans, Suranne Jones , Adrian Lester, Himesh Patel, David Tennant and Jodie Whittaker over 12 weeks in the summer of 2019 as they rehearse songs for their album, Got It Covered. Each actor must choose a song that has special meaning for them and must make their cover version their own. The participants also visit some charity projects that Children in Need supports in order to help raise funds and awareness(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: For this week's task, the candidates head to the historic university cities of Oxford and Cambridge to procure nine items that are associated with both places. The added difficulty for the candidates is that they are given no clues as to which of the cities the various items are located in. In Oxford, timewasting leads to a baffling back-and-forth for one team, while in Cambridge, the other team gamble with their negotiations. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: First Time Doctors
programme.desc: Following a group of newly qualified doctors as they joined hospitals in Northern Ireland's Western Health Trust for the first time. In the first episode, cameras follow Dr Kevin Harkin, who has recently completed his studies in Liverpool. Elsewhere, London-born Dr Josh Walker relishes in his new role and his new surroundings(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy take the children out trick or treating for Halloween, but the night soon turns disastrous for Lee when he loses his phone on the porch of a spooky Victorian house. Once he returns to the house to retrieve his phone, Lee discovers that the house is full of secrets that would be far better left undisturbed(n)

programme.title: Mary Shelley's Frankenstein (1994)
programme.desc: An idealistic but obsessive scientist builds a living creature from the body parts of corpses, then abandons his creation to its fate. However, the monster survives, and tracks his creator down to make a sinister request - with terrifying repercussions if his demands are not met. Gothic horror, directed by and starring Kenneth Branagh. With Robert De Niro and Helena Bonham Carter(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: In Peckham, the body of a 39-year-old man is found slumped in a passageway with a single stab wound to the heart. The documentary follows Met officers as evidence leads them up to a flat where they find six more men, including one lying injured in bed. But is any of them the culprit? Meanwhile, the Community Safety Unit investigate a woman's allegations that she has been emotionally, physically and financially abused by her friend's father, and in Ealing, officers Naomi and Mahad deal with a frequent caller who has called seven times over the past week to complain about her husband(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: Around 72 police officers are attacked every day in England and Wales, meaning they have to find new ways of protecting themselves. As the Government this month considers tougher penalties for people assaulting police officers and proposes equipping more with Taser stun guns, Adam Shaw goes on the frontline with two police forces. He witnesses first-hand officers getting assaulted and asks just what can be done to protect the Bobby on the beat(n)

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Part two of two. The documentary examines Charles's legacy for the Duchy, as senior staff prepare William for the day that he succeeds his father as the next Duke of Cornwall. The documentary also examines his well-known passion for trees and traditional rural crafts, and how these may shape the Duchy's future(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present a special edition dedicated to the stories of twins separated at birth. Mary Holmes and Elaine Allin were born just after the Second World War and raised in the same neighbourhood in Chesterfield, but forbidden from seeing each other. programme.desc: Arthur is attracted to Beryl, the clippie on his regular bus. However, she has only two interests in life - men with a fondness for athletics, and those with a good head of hair. Reg Varney stars(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin uses the threat of a roving mouse as an excuse to spend the night in Chrissy's bedroom. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Vernon buys a batch of topsoil for his new compost-producing business, little suspecting it is from a contaminated landfill site. The shady dealer who sold him the soil winds up dead - as do a number of local cows - and Vernon is soon fighting for his life. Elsewhere, seductive sergeant Nokes sets her sights on Steve Crane, and Bellamy wonders whether Gina will return to Aidensfield and marry him. Geoffrey Hughes, James Carlton and Mark Jordon star(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Elizabeth makes a puzzling discovery, while Annie's actions shock her sons. At Home Farm, Zoe blames Frank for Archie's arrest, and Chris continues to plot against his father(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert refuses to let go of his new friend, there is a reconciliation for Kathy and Kim, and a treat in store for Turner on his birthday(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike is annoyed that Steve has lost his licence, Joe urges Sally to leave Kevin and move in with him. Denise confronts Ivy and tells her she knows she has been making the calls(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Denise posts a cheque through Don's door for the money she owes him. Vera is thrilled to have Terry home and looks forward to having a happy Christmas(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A philandering husband is found dying by the swimming pool after being shot during a house party at the home of Lady Lucy Angkatell. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The killing of a postman, whose doorstep deliveries often included more than just letters, rocks the idyllic rural hamlet of Goodman's Land and sends Barnaby on the trail of a murderer with vengeance in mind - if Sgt Troy can keep his eyes off the attractive village policewoman long enough to follow the clues. Murder mystery, guest starring Robert Glenister, with John Nettles, Daniel Casey and Gillian Kearney(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A six-month-old boy dies in what appears to be a case of cot death, but when shaken baby syndrome is later revealed as the cause, Brooks and Devlin are left with a murder investigation that throws up many possible suspects. New evidence then comes to light during the trial, casting doubt over the prosecution's case, with Steel and Phillips forced into doing some detective work of their own. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A body is found with a note reading 'They Must Be Destroyed' - a chilling reminder of a case Brooks was involved in six years previously, when extreme racist Andrew Dillon killed three boys from ethnic minorities. Steel is then put under intense scrutiny when it is revealed that his success in convicting Dillon was largely due to selective prosecution. Bradley Walsh and Ben Daniels star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
