Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bellamy chases joyriders tearing through Aidensfield and accidentally runs down Don Dibley's son during the pursuit. The boy's father demands compensation, but soon realises his problems have just begun as his daughter reveals she is pregnant. Steve finds himself in trouble and David invents a novel way to rid the estate of moles. Rural drama, starring Mark Jordon, James Carlton and Ted Robbins(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: A new family arrive in style, causing a stir in the village. At Emmerdale, Joe is suspicious of Michael and Rachel doubts Lynn, while at Home Farm, there is no one Frank can trust(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel gives Jay his marching orders, Joe gives Mark a dressing down, and the village is scandalised by the newcomers' plans for the village shop(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera urges Jack to be supportive of Terry. Andy asks his parents to act like a family and all spend Christmas together. Tanya tells Bet about seeing Steve with another girl(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ivy tells Gail about Don pestering Denise, Vera is stunned when Terry explains he wants Tommy to live with the Hortons, and Deirdre is pleased when Ken kisses her(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian sleuth goes on an Egyptian cruise on board an old steamer, but when an American heiress is murdered, his little grey cells go into overdrive. On questioning each of his fellow passengers, he realises many of them have secrets they want to remain hidden. David Suchet stars, with James Fox, Judy Parfitt, Emily Blunt, Daisy Donovan, Frances de la Tour and David Soul(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bellamy chases joyriders tearing through Aidensfield and accidentally runs down Don Dibley's son during the pursuit. The boy's father demands compensation, but soon realises his problems have just begun as his daughter reveals she is pregnant. Steve finds himself in trouble and David invents a novel way to rid the estate of moles. Rural drama, starring Mark Jordon, James Carlton and Ted Robbins(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. Arriving to investigate, Poirot delves back into the annals of local life - and uncovers a series of mysterious deaths, any one of which could have a bearing on the present case. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: An idyllic summer day in Oxford is torn asunder when a parcel bomb claims the life of eminent mathematician Adam Capstone, a genius in the field of 'Knot Theory'. As Lewis and Hathaway investigate, they discover that, while the victim's academic reputation was beyond reproach, his private life was far more dubious. It emerges Capstone was a man of carnal appetites, who had a string of affairs with young students, betraying his chemistry professor wife Elizabeth, providing her with a clear motive to do away with her cheating spouse. As the detectives work to identify the killer bomber, the case becomes a personal challenge for Lewis, who fears he may not be rewarded with another contract should he fail to impress Moody before his six-month trip to New Zealand. Detective drama, starring Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox, with Zoe Tapper, Tristam Summers and Oliver Lansley(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate a hospital department where three patients have died in suspicious circumstances in six months - the latest being a woman brought into A&E with flu-like symptoms. The detectives learn she died after being given a lethal cocktail of prescription drugs - but with an unsigned patient chart and an extremely busy evening on the ward, tracking down the killer proves challenging. Crime drama, guest starring James Fox, with Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A man accused of murdering five people is killed in prison shortly before going to trial, wrapping up a lot of loose ends and saving the state the expense of keeping him behind bars indefinitely. Alas, Morse cannot help wondering why the body of one of his victims was never found - and the chance discovery of a corpse gives him the perfect opportunity to investigate further. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: A nostalgic Stan remembers his first day at the depot, when he was a conductor and Blakey was a driver. He also recounts the events leading to Arthur and Olive's engagement. Comedy, starring Reg Varney, Stephen Lewis and Michael Robbins(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Mr Roper plans to convert the flats into smaller dwelling units - but he has not bargained for Robin, Chrissy and Jo having a three-year lease. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian sleuth goes on an Egyptian cruise on board an old steamer, but when an American heiress is murdered, his little grey cells go into overdrive. David Suchet stars, with James Fox, Judy Parfitt, Emily Blunt, Daisy Donovan, Frances de la Tour and David Soul(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A recently widowed woman arrives in Aidensfield wanting to bury her husband in the family grave, but the deceased's brother refuses to co-operate because of an old feud. Vernon and David get involved and find themselves held at gunpoint as a shocking case of fraud is uncovered. Rural drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes and David Lonsdale(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank cannot believe what Zoe is telling him, Pollard panics when Elizabeth reveals her plan, and Archie's request gives Rachel the wrong idea(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: At the barbecue, Rachel loses a friend, Vic is fuming when Scott loses his balance, and Chris is lost for words when he is finally cut down to size(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel and Bet do battle at the sales, Vera tries to contact the Hortons in vain, and Ivy reports Don as a missing person to the police(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack is concerned for Vera as she refuses to eat or sleep, Des receives his decree nisi, Don contacts Bet to see how Ivy is, and Vicky wants Steve to go away with her(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth finds himself in interesting company as English socialites and American millionaires rub shoulders on board a train bound for the French Riviera. Passengers include oil magnate Rufus Van Aldin (Elliott Gould) and his daughter Ruth (Jaime Murray) - to whom he has given a magnificent ruby known as the Heart of Fire. With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A recently widowed woman arrives in Aidensfield wanting to bury her husband in the family grave, but the deceased's brother refuses to co-operate because of an old feud. Vernon and David get involved and find themselves held at gunpoint as a shocking case of fraud is uncovered. Rural drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes and David Lonsdale(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A lavish dinner party proves to be a prelude to murder and intrigue - but fortunately Poirot is among the guests, and soon realises the key to the mystery lies in a duel fought 10 years previously. Drama, starring David Suchet, Caroline Langrishe and John McEnery(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A local woman is killed when an experiment in biological warfare goes tragically wrong. Sam also falls victim to a potentially fatal illness as a result of the project. Foyle is forced to head into the most secretive and dangerous areas of wartime research, unearthing secrets that cannot be revealed even to the prime minister. Michael Kitchen stars, with Honeysuckle Weeks and Philip Franks(n)

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alf refuses to honour the OAP vouchers Brendan issued, Deirdre feels helpless as Tracy packs her bags and leaves with Craig, and Vera visits Terry in prison for the last time(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin goes to court, assuring Sally that he will be let off lightly. Don advises Denise to stop the search for the caller as the police might get involved(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy discovers Chris's deception, Michael finds he is unwelcome at Emmerdale, and Pollard and Elizabeth return home to an unpleasant surprise(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy makes a decision, and Rachel makes an angry move. Unknown to the Sugdens, Annie and Leonard are making plans for the future(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An elderly man reports an unusual break-in, during which the uninvited visitor left a mysterious statue on the kitchen table. It soon emerges that it is just the first in a series of unexplained events, as a police phone box arrives and then disappears from his garden, cement is delivered to his house for no apparent reason and a fire is started in his garage. Noakes advises Steve to keep well away as it is not technically a police matter, but the officer decides to investigate further. Rural drama, starring Trevor Ray, Georgie Glen and James Carlton(n)

As the detectives work to identify the killer bomber, the case becomes a personal challenge for Lewis, who fears he may not be rewarded with another contract should he fail to impress Moody before his six-month trip to New Zealand. Detective drama, starring Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox, with Zoe Tapper, Tristam Summers and Oliver Lansley(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate a hospital department where three patients have died in suspicious circumstances in six months - the latest being a woman brought into A&E with flu-like symptoms. The detectives learn she died after being given a lethal cocktail of prescription drugs - but with an unsigned patient chart and an extremely busy evening on the ward, tracking down the killer proves challenging. Crime drama, guest starring James Fox, with Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A man accused of murdering five people is killed in prison shortly before going to trial, wrapping up a lot of loose ends and saving the state the expense of keeping him behind bars indefinitely. Alas, Morse cannot help wondering why the body of one of his victims was never found - and the chance discovery of a corpse gives him the perfect opportunity to investigate further. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera urges Jack to be supportive of Terry. Andy asks his parents to act like a family and all spend Christmas together. Tanya tells Bet about seeing Steve with another girl(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ivy tells Gail about Don pestering Denise, Vera is stunned when Terry explains he wants Tommy to live with the Hortons, and Deirdre is pleased when Ken kisses her(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: A new family arrive in style, causing a stir in the village. At Emmerdale, Joe is suspicious of Michael and Rachel doubts Lynn, while at Home Farm, there is no one Frank can trust(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel gives Jay his marching orders, Joe gives Mark a dressing down, and the village is scandalised by the newcomers' plans for the village shop(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bellamy chases joyriders tearing through Aidensfield and accidentally runs down Don Dibley's son during the pursuit. The boy's father demands compensation, but soon realises his problems have just begun as his daughter reveals she is pregnant. Steve finds himself in trouble and David invents a novel way to rid the estate of moles. Rural drama, starring Mark Jordon, James Carlton and Ted Robbins(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan overspends at the dog track and is unable to pay his housekeeping money, so hits on the idea of using the bus to give the senior citizens a day out in return for cash. Comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: A nostalgic Stan remembers his first day at the depot, when he was a conductor and Blakey was a driver. He also recounts the events leading to Arthur and Olive's engagement. Comedy, starring Reg Varney, Stephen Lewis and Michael Robbins(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Mr Roper plans to convert the flats into smaller dwelling units - but he has not bargained for Robin, Chrissy and Jo having a three-year lease. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian sleuth goes on an Egyptian cruise on board an old steamer, but when an American heiress is murdered, his little grey cells go into overdrive. On questioning each of his fellow passengers, he realises many of them have secrets they want to remain hidden. David Suchet stars, with James Fox, Judy Parfitt, Emily Blunt, Daisy Donovan, Frances de la Tour and David Soul(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A recently widowed woman arrives in Aidensfield wanting to bury her husband in the family grave, but the deceased's brother refuses to co-operate because of an old feud. Vernon and David get involved and find themselves held at gunpoint as a shocking case of fraud is uncovered. Rural drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes and David Lonsdale(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank cannot believe what Zoe is telling him, Pollard panics when Elizabeth reveals her plan, and Archie's request gives Rachel the wrong idea(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: At the barbecue, Rachel loses a friend, Vic is fuming when Scott loses his balance, and Chris is lost for words when he is finally cut down to size(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel and Bet do battle at the sales, Vera tries to contact the Hortons in vain, and Ivy reports Don as a missing person to the police(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack is concerned for Vera as she refuses to eat or sleep, Des receives his decree nisi, Don contacts Bet to see how Ivy is, and Vicky wants Steve to go away with her(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth finds himself in interesting company as English socialites and American millionaires rub shoulders on board a train bound for the French Riviera. Passengers include oil magnate Rufus Van Aldin (Elliott Gould) and his daughter Ruth (Jaime Murray) - to whom he has given a magnificent ruby known as the Heart of Fire. With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A recently widowed woman arrives in Aidensfield wanting to bury her husband in the family grave, but the deceased's brother refuses to co-operate because of an old feud. Vernon and David get involved and find themselves held at gunpoint as a shocking case of fraud is uncovered. Rural drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes and David Lonsdale(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A lavish dinner party proves to be a prelude to murder and intrigue - but fortunately Poirot is among the guests, and soon realises the key to the mystery lies in a duel fought 10 years previously. Drama, starring David Suchet, Caroline Langrishe and John McEnery(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A local woman is killed when an experiment in biological warfare goes tragically wrong. Sam also falls victim to a potentially fatal illness as a result of the project. Foyle is forced to head into the most secretive and dangerous areas of wartime research, unearthing secrets that cannot be revealed even to the prime minister. Michael Kitchen stars, with Honeysuckle Weeks and Philip Franks(n)

programme.title: Only When I Laugh
programme.desc: New admission Harry Bridgewater turns out to be a convict who has come in for an operation with a prison guard in tow. However, he soon pressures Figgis, Glover and Norman to cover his absence while he enjoys a spot of overnight passion with his wife. Comedy, starring James Bolam(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: The Saint is framed for the murder of a young sculptor, and with the help of the victim's girlfriend, Chief Inspector Teal fights to clear his name and uncover the real culprit. Adventure, starring Roger Moore(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A criminal poses as a priest to avoid suspicion while masterminding a heist during a jeweler's convention. But to escape justice, he needs to murder one of his partners in crime(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A car accident victim appears to have suffered previous injuries - leading the medical examiner to bring an abuse charge against the 9-year-old boy's mother(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry protects a widowed garage owner from her grasping family and Arthur spots a chance to achieve one of his greatest ambitions - getting his hands on a Rolls-Royce. Lionel Jeffries and Keith Barron guest star alongside Dennis Waterman and George Cole(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Suspected felon Billy Medhurst returns from Spain, and Regan determines to prove his involvement in an earlier robbery in which a Sweeney office was shot and left permanently disabled. Police drama, starring John Thaw, Dennis Waterman and Anthony Heaton(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: The brother of a suspected drug-dealer dies while in custody, and his elder sibling directs his powerful lawyer to instigate a committee to investigate CI5's involvement. With the future of the division in question, Bodie and Doyle try to prove the family's drug-trade connection and the real reason behind the man's death. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar joins forces with a glamorous female agent in order to help liberate John Spring, a double agent whom the Russians believe to be working for them. Roger Moore stars(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo teaches a criminal justice course at a college, and one of his students tries to frame him by stealing items from him and leaving them at crime scenes. James Woods guest stars(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A priest known for his crusade against an obscene publication is found dead in a prostitute's bedroom, drawing Quincy into an intriguing investigation(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry gets a job chauffeuring the villainous Micky the Fish, but fears for his life when the gangster's mistress reports her jewels missing. Light-hearted crime caper, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: An Israeli minister is abducted, and the ensuing suburban siege triggers an airborne pursuit as Bodie and Doyle try to save the day. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan is joined by an old friend - Turkish policeman Captain Shebbeq - to help him track down a gold-smuggling ring. As he observes a secret government deal to transport the precious commodity, he comes to suspect there is something rather sinister also within the cargo. Nadim Sawalha guest stars, with John Thaw(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Thousands of pounds in prize-money donations to charity are up for grabs once again, as Bradley Walsh invites TV presenter Jamie Theakston, singer-songwriter and actress Faye Tozer, retired England rugby player Ben Cohen and comedienne Sara Pascoe to take part in the quiz. To win, they must work as a team to prove their general knowledge know-how by beating one of the show's resident experts at their own game(n)

Drama sequel, starring Catherine Bell and Chris Potter(n)

programme.title: How the Victorians Built Britain
programme.desc: Presenter Michael Buerk looks at how the Victorians created what is now known as the modern home, exploring the huge rise in house-building during the period. He travels to Fakenham, Norfolk, to visit the last remaining gasworks in England, and discovers how the Victorians mastered the art of producing 'town gas' from coal. He also investigates how the kitchen was transformed with the advent of gas cookers, as more complex meals including the Sunday roast steadily became the norm across the nation(n)

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of a high-speed police interception unit. Officers Nick and Bob chase down a suspicious looking van with false registration plates. programme.title: Police Raids: Caught by Surprise
programme.desc: Cameras follow enforcement agencies carrying out raids across Britain, as they take drastic action against fraudsters, counterfeiters and criminals. A specialist counterfeit raid team targets businesses suspected of dealing fake goods, and energy theft enforcers raid a Liverpool takeaway which has registered a sudden drop in gas usage(n)

programme.title: Undercover Girlfriends

In the third episode, The girls turn detective when they discover a secret text message has been sent, and all of the men could be involved. Later, they decide to call the boys from the middle of a nightclub(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Botched Up Bodies

programme.title: The Sex Business

programme.title: How the Victorians Built Britain
programme.desc: Michael Buerk looks at the creation of mass manufacturing, which meant that goods could be produced at a rate that had never been seen before. In Manchester, he learns how engineer George Stephenson created the first intercity railway, so that cotton could be transported quickly and cheaply from Liverpool docks to Manchester's mills. Michael also discovers how the sewing machine - newly created in 1851 - created the rag trade, and visits what was probably the first shopping mall in the UK, the Burlington Arcade in London(n)

programme.title: The Big Con?
programme.desc: Paul Connolly and Jean Johansson present a consumer show investigating claims made by shops and advertisers. In the first edition, they examine pre-prepared food, meeting a Birmingham family whose two ready-meal mad kids can't get enough of the microwave. But will they still be fans when Paul shows them some of the things that may be lurking in their lunch? The team also looks at the bacteria that might be hiding inside some of the most popular convenience foods used by millions of families across the UK(n)

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: A retired couple and a single mother from Swindon are the latest to swap homes, lives and budgets to sample life on the other side of the tracks. Shahid and Riffat have worked all their lives planning for their retirement. They have built their own home and live off a comfortable pension. Kelly lives in a three-bedroom council house with her sons. She works as a cleaner and spends no money on herself, with everything going to her sons. How will both parties be affected by the swap?(n)

programme.title: Undercover Girlfriends

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee deals with his father's recent death by writing him a letter outlining the details of his latest investigation. The case involved tracking down a hactivist collective known as Krampus, who were thought to have been responsible for an incident in which the internet crashed all over Washington, DC. McGee and Abby brought in three known cyber-terrorists for questioning, and discover evidence that links the trio to an attempted breach of the Congress e-mail server and archive. However, when this line of inquiry reaches a dead-end, the team picks up a new lead that suggests an isolated hacker from Wisconsin may be responsible. This 'lone wolf' scenario does not seem plausible to McGee, though, as he recalls a lesson his late father taught him. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy lieutenant is murdered at the Defence Logistics Agency, while visitings the office of an admiral currently away at sea. As the team investigates the scene of the crime, they realise a rare painting found in the admiral's office is actually an elaborate fake placed there after the killing, replacing the stolen original. As the quest to locate the missing artwork gets under way, the agents realise they need the help of an expert. Luckily, Tony's con-artist father has returned to town, having broken up with his fiancee, and is on hand to provide expert advice as the hunt for the killer takes them into the shady world of the black market. Guest starring Robert Wagner(n)

programme.title: The Gadget Show
programme.desc: Members of the G Team pitch the latest survival tech to former Royal Marine sniper Aldo Kane, while Jon Bentley pounds the pavements with a TV journalist for the day to test out the latest big screen smartphones. Over in California, Georgie Barrat discovers why the electric scooter company's success hasn't yet made it this side of the pond, before Ortis Deley gets an exclusive try himself during their first UK trials. Plus, this week's Wall of Fame sees Jon pitch for the UK's best-selling computer of all time, the Raspberry Pi. Presented by Craig Charles(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Cameras capture a route through Spain, beginning near Leon, part of the famous north Spanish Pilgrimage, in the Picos de Europa mountains. The railway then runs through the Basque country before the famous Guggenheim Museum greets travellers in Bilbao. In Galicia, the gorges, coastline and sea bridges are a sight for sore eyes, and a speciality cider tour refreshes the train's guests. The journey ends in Ferrol, a spectacular harbour that has been a major shipbuilding centre for most of its history and has been a key city in many wars. Narrated by Bill Nighy(n)

programme.title: Caravanning with Shane Richie
programme.desc: The presenter visits a vast campsite in North Yorkshire, which has its own theme park and zoo. The place is so big that guests can call for a golf-buggy taxi just to take them to their room. Once, settled in, Shane hops on the cable cars for an aerial view of the park's rollercoasters and wild animal enclosures. He also explores the local area, taking in a steam train trip from inside the driver's cabin and a fishing trip out of Whitby. Following in his father's footsteps as an performer, Shane's son arrives with his band to perform as the site's entertainment(n)

programme.title: Undercover Girlfriends
programme.desc: The women confront their partners one-on-one after having meticulously planned their final showdowns, with the others watching on as each face-off with a shocked boyfriend occurs. Some couples are happily reunited, while others end on a different page altogether(n)

programme.title: The Best of Bad TV
programme.desc: The return of the documentary celebrating those moments when events on the small screen went wrong in the most spectacular ways imaginable. The highlights - or rather lowlights - include TV cook Fanny Cradock taking a competition winner to task for creating a dish that did not live up to her exacting standards, and a variety show co-starring Formula One drivers - giving James Hunt a chance to literally blow his own trumpet. There are also various talent show cock-ups, including a memorable moment when a microphone failed during Fame Academy, and plenty of soaps, music and drama disasters too, from tears in Changing Rooms to a talking dog in That's Life!(n)

programme.title: Beau Geste (1939)
programme.desc: Three brothers are accused of stealing from their aristocratic adopted mother. They seek to salvage their honour by joining the Foreign Legion, battling a rampaging Arab army as well as coming into conflict with their sadistic commanding officer. Action adventure, starring Gary Cooper, Ray Milland, Robert Preston and Brian Donlevy(n)

programme.title: 3:10 to Yuma (1957)
programme.desc: A farmer in need of money agrees to guard a notorious outlaw until the train arrives to transport him to jail. Meanwhile, the pair are holed up in a hotel, where his prisoner plays mind games with him - and the villain's cohorts are waiting outside ready to engineer an escape. Western adapted from a novel by Elmore Leonard, with Van Heflin, Glenn Ford, Felicia Farr and Leora Dana(n)

programme.title: 40 Guns to Apache Pass (1967)
programme.desc: A cavalry captain sets out to deliver a shipment of rifles to a fort besieged by marauding Apaches, but the mission is undermined by a villainous corporal who plans to steal the weapons and sell them to the enemy. Western, starring Audie Murphy, Michael Burns, Laraine Stephens, Kenneth Tobey and Michael Keep(n)

programme.title: Carry On Teacher (1959)
programme.desc: A headmaster hopes to land a more prestigious job at a better school, but his pupils do not want him to leave. When two inspectors pay a visit, the mischievous students see a chance to sabotage his career plans with a series of pranks. Comedy, starring Ted Ray, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey and Kenneth Williams(n)

programme.title: Footloose (2011)
programme.desc: A teenager from Boston moves to a small conservative town, and discovers the council and a preacher have enforced a ban on loud music and dancing. He openly defies the harsh rules, revitalising the local youth and winning the heart of the reverend's daughter. Remake of the 1984 drama, with Kenny Wormald, Julianne Hough and Dennis Quaid(n)

programme.title: Deadpool (2016)
programme.desc: A fast-talking former mercenary develops terminal cancer, and agrees to be the subject in a shady experiment in the hope of a cure. The procedure leaves him horribly disfigured, but also possessing superhuman abilities which he immediately puts to use in a quest for revenge on the man responsible for his hideous appearance. Superhero comedy adventure, starring Ryan Reynolds and Morena Baccarin(n)

programme.title: The Midnight Man (2016)
programme.desc: A teenager and her friend are thrown into a living nightmare when a game they discover in an attic releases a terrifying creature. Horror, starring Robert Englund(n)

programme.title: Random Acts
programme.desc: Surreal, eerie and visually striking fairytale, directed by Dylan Holmes Williams. A young man is trapped in an oppressive dystopia where everybody must wear ginormous metal stilts(n)

programme.title: Fright Night (2011)
programme.desc: A teenager realises his new neighbour is a vampire, but cannot convince anyone of the danger they are in. He turns to a foul-mouthed magician with a horror-themed Las Vegas show to help him slay the bloodsucker before he murders the entire neighbourhood. Comedy horror remake, with Anton Yelchin, Colin Farrell, David Tennant and Toni Collette(n)

programme.title: The Gunfight at Dodge City (1959)
programme.desc: A gambler becomes the sheriff of a frontier town, planning to use his position to avenge his brother's murderers - but the arrival of a face from the past muddies the water, and leaves him doubting the identity of the killer. Western, starring Joel McCrea, Julie Adams, John McIntire, Nancy Gates and Richard Anderson(n)

programme.title: Hell Below Zero (1954)
programme.desc: A thrill-seeking explorer joins a whaling ship and is persuaded by the female captain to investigate the disappearance of her father on an earlier expedition. The pair become suspicious of her former lover - and are proved right when he leaves them for dead in Antarctic waters. Action adventure, starring Alan Ladd, Joan Tetzel, Basil Sydney and Stanley Baker(n)

programme.title: A Man Alone (1955)
programme.desc: A gunman stumbles across the bodies of passengers murdered in a stagecoach robbery. Riding to the nearest town to report the crime, he finds himself accused of the killings and faces a battle to prove his innocence. Western, directed by and starring Ray Milland, with Raymond Burr, Lee Van Cleef, Mary Murphy and Ward Bond(n)

programme.title: Robinson Crusoe on Mars (1964)
programme.desc: Stranded on Mars with only a monkey as a companion, an astronaut has to quickly figure out how to survive a lack of oxygen, food and water on the lifeless planet. Sci-fi drama, inspired by Daniel Defoe's classic tale, starring Paul Mantee, Adam West and Victor Lundin(n)

programme.title: The Host (2013)
programme.desc: Earth is invaded by parasitic aliens which possess humans, but one of the creatures discovers the consciousness of her teenage host has not been fully repressed. The human part of their personality persuades her to turn her back on her race and seek out free humans, leading her to a resistance group fighting to free mankind. Sci-fi adventure, starring Saoirse Ronan, Max Irons and William Hurt(n)

programme.title: 47 Metres Down (2017)
programme.desc: Two sisters vacationing in Mexico are trapped in a shark cage at the bottom of the ocean. With less than an hour of oxygen left and great white sharks circling nearby, they must fight to survive. Horror, starring Mandy Moore, Claire Holt and Matthew Modine(n)

programme.title: Drag Me to Hell (2009)
programme.desc: A bank employee refuses a mortgage extension to an elderly woman, who responds by placing her under a curse. As she endures a series of unexplained misfortunes, she discovers a demon has been sent after her, and if she cannot break the spell in three days, it will take her to Hell. programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton visits Vietnam, where a global surge in demand for pepper has transformed the lives of farmers across the country. She learns the difference between black and white varieties and the secrets of peppercorn quality control, and discovers the remarkable lengths to which producers go to guard their valuable crop from thieves. Jimmy Doherty meets a man whose job entails harvesting different strains of yeast from locations around the world and Matt Tebbutt looks into how manufacturers flavour crisps(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt heads to Italy to find out how supermarkets can sell pesto at an affordable price when the traditional ingredients are expensive, while Jimmy Doherty investigates why so many cockles in UK supermarkets come from abroad when he always thought of them as a quintessentially British staple. Kate Quilton takes a trip around Europe's largest marshmallow factory to find out why the sticky treat goes crispy and melty at the same time over the campfire(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away

programme.desc: Richard Fall and Laura Howes are thinking of settling down to start a family, but are torn between buying a large home in Wakefield, West Yorkshire, near where they live, or purchasing an apartment in Valencia on Spain's eastern coast(n)

programme.desc: Edinburgh security company manager Anne hopes her homely, relaxed night in will be enough to win the £1,000 prize - but rising tensions between the guests may scupper her chances(n)

However, after the previous night's tension, his main concern is keeping any potential explosions contained. Guests Anne and Larah have a suspicion he could be serious competition. Will James manage to produce excellent food and keep the peace, leading him to victory?(n)

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: In Brixworth, Northamptonshire, rugby fan Ian wants to leave the property that he and wife Sarah bought together two years ago. With a toddler and a couple of dogs, their two-bed rural semi is bursting at the seams. However, Sarah believes the house has the potential to cater for a growing family and wants to stay - if they can sort out their dingy ground floor. Kirstie wants to open up the downstairs and drive a digger through the conservatory, but will Ian and Sarah like the idea?(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Labrador is rushed to Fitzpatrick's unable to stand, and although Professor Noel Fitzpatrick tells the family that surgery is not an option, his team devises an alternative care plan. The vets also treat a bichon frise suffering chronic elbow pain, and a leonberger has a bone tumour removed from its leg after being diagnosed with cancer(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets a master craftsman whose dream is to build a castle in rural Devon made entirely of mud. Kevin McCabe plans to form the huge property out of two vast curved roundhouses - the largest of which is inspired by the natural geometry of a snail shell - connected by glazing and topped with wildflower meadow roofs to mirror the surrounding countryside. However, the project involves mixing and laying a mind-boggling 2,000 tons of cob, all during warm, sunny weather - and the sheer scale of the task soon becomes clear(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: The wedding season draws to close, but Dick and Angel need to look ahead to the winter. Dick ploughs up the wild flower meadow, a change that Angel does not entirely approve of, and the couple transforms the dilapidated coach house into a cafe, complete with a striking neon sign(n)

programme.title: The Team
programme.desc: Seven people are murdered during a brutal shootout at a remote bed and breakfast. The only survivor of the massacre is a young Syrian woman, who escapes into the vast surrounding marshland. The victims of Marsh Farm also include some illegal migrants who had evidently gone into hiding there. A student from Brussels who had previously been in contact with the Caliphate Union terrorist organisation and acted as an informer for the Belgian secret service, is also killed during the attack. Inspector Nelly Winther from Denmark, Hamburg investigator Gregor Weiss and Belgian secret service officer Paula Liekens spring into action to find those responsible. Crime thriller, starring Jurgen Vogel, Marie Bach Hansen and Lynn Van Royen. In English, German, Flemish, French and Danish(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: In an edition of the documentary focusing on fathers and sons, 51-year-old builder Rod is rushed into A&E after falling 18 feet from scaffolding onto concrete. When scans reveal that he has fractured some of his vertebrae and will require extensive surgery, his wife Nicky is left to call their two sons to prepare them for bad news. Meanwhile, 86-year-old retired policeman John is brought to St George's with a head injury after falling off a wall. As doctors assess the severity of the damage, he talks about how his career took over his life, so much so that he missed the birth of his son. Plus, 19-year-old cyclist Mack requires specialist help for a dislocated shoulder, after he collided with a Porsche. His dad explains how adopting him and his brother changed all of their lives(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Three-year-old Louie is airlifted to St George's after being run over by a car while playing outside, with doctors expressing concern about his neck, which seems to be stuck in one position. Meanwhile, 41-year-old Sam arrives in resus with a cut to her head and possible spinal and internal injuries after losing control of her car and crashing into a tree(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May attempt to improve the ambulance, with the presenters all going their own separate ways to enhance one area they think could do with an overhaul. James focuses on passenger comfort, Richard tries to shorten response times by getting other drivers out of the way more effectively, an issue Jeremy tackles with the invention of what he calls the 'sports ambulance'. Their three very different creations are then put to the test with a series of severe and often messy challenges. Plus, Formula One driver Daniel Ricciardo is the Star in the Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look at the Airbus A380, which as the largest passenger plane ever built can carry more than 850 people non-stop nearly half-way around the world. With the largest wingspan of any commercial aircraft and four specially developed mighty turbo jet engines, the A380 weighs 560 tonnes and getting it off the ground would not have been possible without the work of past innovators such as George Cayley and Frank Whittle. Key developments in aerospace engineering - from Richard Whitcombe's energy-efficient wing-tip designs to Nasa engineers' cutting-edge control system, Fly by Wire - helped bring the A380 to fruition(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: How animals in the polar regions adapt to the summer weather. In a colony of 400,000 king penguins the adults go surfing and the chicks take mud-baths to cool off, while polar bear cubs take their first swimming lessons as the ice they inhabit melts away. A minke whale is hunted by a family of killer whales, succumbing to their battering after two hours, and Adelie penguins try to prevent their chicks being snatched by skuas(n)

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: An examination of the dark history behind Britain's fascination with murder, beginning with historian and curator Lucy Worsley exploring how notorious killings were transformed into popular entertainment in the first half of the 19th century(n)

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: In the second programme, the historian explores how science and detection influenced the popular culture of murder during Victorian times. Writers including Charles Dickens and Wilkie Collins were fascinated by grisly crimes, and the literary genre that came out of it captured the imagination of readers. The presenter also reveals that when Jack the Ripper began his reign of terror in London at the same time Robert Louis Stevenson's Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde was published, the idea of the serial killer was introduced to the British public(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team head to Swansea to investigate the history of White Rock, which was one of the first copper-smelting works. As the archaeologists strip turf and shift tons of earth, they reveal the traces of a once-great industry and rediscover the story of the men who worked in it(n)

programme.title: Madagascar
programme.desc: An observation of the wildlife that inhabits the narrow band of mountains and rainforest on the island's east coast, from ring-tailed lemurs living on an inhospitable peak, to forest-dwelling sifakas, small primates on the verge of extinction. The programme also focuses on the golden bamboo lemur, which is capable of consuming daily doses of cyanide, and looks at the fruit-rich treetops where red-ruffed lemurs defend their food and territory. Narrated by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines how the arrival of winter sparks a ruthless battle for survival in the polar regions, with temperatures plummeting to -70C, winds reaching 120mph and day-long darkness. As a blizzard rages, a polar bear treks into the Arctic mountains to give birth, while the entire world population of spectacled eider braves the weather in a giant ice hole. Wolves and bison engage in battle, but alliances are also made, and beneath the snow, voles try to dodge the attacks of great grey owls and weasels(n)

programme.title: Titanic: Stories from the Deep
programme.desc: Thanks to the use of cutting-edge technology, a unique collection of artefacts salvaged from the underwater resting site of the famous wreck suggest new stories of love, deception, fate and heroics. This first episode touches on Fifth Officer Harold Lowe. After Titanic sank, his lifeboat was one of the few to go back and pick up survivors. One of the people he saved, Renee Harris, gifted him a set of gold-plated nautical equipment as a symbol of her gratitude. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin(n)

Crime drama, starring Danny Pino, Mariska Hargitay and Richard Belzer(n)

programme.desc: This edition of the documentary about life at Gascoigne Primary School in Barking, east London, follows three very different boys the school supports in various ways. programme.title: Brexit: The Battle for Britain
programme.desc: On June 23, the people of Britain voted to leave the European Union. Within hours of the result, the prime minister had resigned, the pound was falling and Westminster was reeling. How and why did this political revolution happen - and how does it stand to reshape British politics? Here, BBC Political Editor Laura Kuenssberg tells the inside story of how David Cameron's referendum plan backfired and the Leave campaigners won. Talking to the politicians and the people who ran the campaigns, she finds out how events escalated to not only end Cameron's premiership but reshape Britain's place in Europe(n)

programme.desc: A Panorama investigation into the world of family courts, focusing on the cases of parents who have had their children taken away on the crucial evidence of doctors stating that tiny fractures on their babies' X-rays are a sign of abuse. However, some experts now believe there are other explanations, including a lack of vitamin D or rickets. One young mother who suffered the trauma of having her child taken from her has gone abroad to have another baby. It's a drastic step - but one that may not even keep her out of the reach of the social services(n)
